diagrams.title: Process Hierarchy Tree
diagrams.description: Shows the parent-child relationship between processes in Unix/Linux
elements: PID 0 (Swapper/Scheduler) at top as root of kernel
PID 1 (Init) below kernel as first user process
Child processes branching from init and each other
Each process shows PID and PPID
diagrams.purpose: Understanding process creation, parent-child relationships, and special PIDs

diagrams.title: fork() System Call Flow
diagrams.description: Illustrates what happens when fork() is called
elements: fork() call splits into two paths
diagrams.purpose: Understanding process creation and PID handling in fork()

diagrams.title: Zombie Process Formation
diagrams.description: Shows how zombie processes are created
elements: Process shown as 'ZOMBIE' in state column
PID still occupied until parent calls wait()
diagrams.purpose: Understanding zombie process creation and prevention using wait()

diagrams.title: Orphan Process Adoption
diagrams.description: Shows how orphan processes are handled
elements: init performs wait() for adopted processes
diagrams.purpose: Understanding orphan process handling and init's role

diagrams.title: Process, Process Group, and Session
elements: Single process can be group leader
Each level has its ID (PID, PGID, SID)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding process grouping and session organization
